features: Categories can now be reordered — drag a category in the rail and drop it where you want.
On launch the app reopens the last category you had selected, instead of the largest one.
Window size and position are remembered more reliably across quit and reopen, including a maximized window.

Artifact preview drawer: a session's files now open in a fold-down drawer over the terminal — images and SVG render inline, text/CSV/Markdown show as text, and HTML/PDF open in your browser. A clear "close drawer" bar sits at the bottom.
Artifacts are now scoped to what the session itself produced (from its own writes), so sessions sharing a folder no longer show each other's files.
Artifacts now include plain-text and data files (.txt, .csv, .tsv, .log), not just images and docs.
Fixed the 5-hour and 7-day usage readouts bouncing between values — they now reconcile every session's snapshot into one stable current number.
The collapsible section headers (activity, artifacts, timeline, Arbiter) now have a raised background so they're easy to spot.

Artifacts: the companion now surfaces the previewable files the open session produced — images, SVG, PDF, HTML, and Markdown — each with Open (in the default app) and Reveal in Finder. Detected passively from what the session wrote, and only listed while the file still exists.
Renamed the session-timeline pop-out to Timeline (it was Activity, which now belongs to the activity panel).
Note: in-app preview (rendering images/PDF/HTML inside a drawer) is coming in a follow-up — this release surfaces and opens the files.

Activity view now leads with the session you're looking at — its full ledger sits at the top, while every other session collapses to a one-line rollup chip (running / failed / done counts) you can click to switch to it.
Workflow runs show as a single rich entry (name, description, and agent progress like 8/19) instead of a long list of individual agent rows.
Renamed the pop-out panel from Subagents to Activity, since it now covers shell tasks and workflows too.
Activity view: background shell tasks and workflows now appear alongside agents, each with a type badge, and a failed task shows as a quiet awareness row.
Rail rows show a "⚙ N" count of background work running, so a session churning on a build or dev server no longer looks idle.
Fixed background agents and tasks being marked done the instant they launched — they now reflect real running / done / failed status.
A completed turn now surfaces as a "done — your move" item for sessions you weren't watching, and clears once you open them.
Fixed an "Object has been destroyed" error that could surface when the app quit to install an update. Every update from this version on quits cleanly.
Context-usage bar: a slim bar along the bottom of every session item that fills and shifts from green to amber to red as its context window fills — on the sidebar and the needs-you board.
Configurable terminal font: pick any installed monospace font and size for the terminal in Settings, applied live. Only fonts that will actually render are offered, with a live preview.
The post-update summary now only appears in installed builds, not when running from source.
Automatic updates: the app checks on launch and once a day, shows an update window with per-release notes and the full changelog, and can install now or on quit.
The window now remembers its position and size across restarts.
Added Settings and Check for Updates to the app menu, plus a Check for updates link in the About window.
After an update installs, a one-time summary shows what changed in the new version.
Usage readouts now show rounded percentages instead of long floating-point values.
Fixed a spawned child inheriting a category from its folder instead of from its edge to the parent.
Fixed the subagent fleet panel clipping its contents in a docked layout.
Keep the Uncategorized rail entry always visible so it stops flickering.
Modals now scroll internally instead of overflowing a small or non-fullscreen window.
Usage readouts: live context-window percentage plus 5-hour and 7-day rate-limit meters.
